from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Helper dictionaries for phrasing
    simple_phrases = {
        "birthDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born on {o}",
        "birthYear": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born in {o}",
        "deathDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} died on {o}",
        "deathPlace": lambda s, o: f"{s} died in {o}",
        "birthPlace": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born in {o}",
        "origin": lambda s, o: f"{s} originates from {o}",
        "nationality": lambda s, o: f"{s} is {o}",
        "occupation": lambda s, o: f"{s} works as {o}",
        "background": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a background as {o}",
        "activeYearsStartYear": lambda s, o: f"{s} started their career in {o}",
        "activeYearsEndYear": lambda s, o: f"{s} ended their career in {o}",
        "instrument": lambda s, o: f"{s} plays {o}",
        "genre": lambda s, o: f"{s} is a {o} musician",
        "recordLabel": lambda s, o: f"{s} is signed to {o}",
        "associatedBand/associatedMusicalArtist": lambda s, o: f"{s} is associated with {o}",
        "bandMember": lambda s, o: f"{s} includes member {o}",
        "parentCompany": lambda s, o: f"{s} is owned by {o}",
        "location": lambda s, o: f"{s} is located in {o}",
        "leader": lambda s, o: f"{s} is led by {o}",
        "capital": lambda s, o: f"{s} has capital {o}",
        "currency": lambda s, o: f"{s} uses the {o}",
        "anthem": lambda s, o: f"{s}'s anthem is {o}",
        "country": lambda s, o: f"{s} is in {o}",
        "isPartOf": lambda s, o: f"{s} is part of {o}",
        "derivative": lambda s, o: f"{o} is derived from {s}",
        "governingBody": lambda s, o: f"{s} is governed by {o}",
        "leaderTitle": lambda s, o: f"{s} has leader titled {o}",
        "language": lambda s, o: f"{s} speaks {o}",
        "officialLanguage": lambda s, o: f"The official language of {s} is {o}",
        "ethnicGroup": lambda s, o: f"The main ethnic group of {s} is {o}",
        "populationDensity": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a population density of {o}",
        "meaning": lambda s, o: f"The meaning of {s} is {o}",
        "longName": lambda s, o: f"{s} is officially called {o}",
        "areaTotal": lambda s, o: f"{s} covers an area of {o}",
        "foundingDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} was founded on {o}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": lambda s, o: f"{s} lies at {o} meters above sea level",
        "postalCode": lambda s, o: f"The postal code of {s} is {o}",
        "musicFusionGenre": lambda s, o: f"{o} is a musical fusion of {s}",
        "musicSubgenre": lambda s, o: f"{o} is a subgenre of {s}",
        "stylisticOrigin": lambda s, o: f"{s} has its stylistic origins in {o}",
    }

    # Group triples by subject for coherent sentences
    from collections import defaultdict
    subject_groups = defaultdict(list)
    for t in triples:
        subject_groups[t.subject].append(t)

    # Collect all genre objects to later possibly skip their own sentences
    genre_objects = {t.object for t in triples if t.predicate == "genre"}

    sentences = []

    # Process each subject group
    # Pre‑compute genre information for subjects that are bands (or any entity)
    band_genres = {t.subject: t.object for t in triples if t.predicate == "genre"}

    for subject, group in subject_groups.items():
        # Skip subjects that are only genre entities used in a genre triple
        if subject in genre_objects and all(t.predicate == "stylisticOrigin" for t in group):
            continue
        # Separate out association triples, genre‑fusion triples and the rest
        assoc_triples = [t for t in group if t.predicate == "associatedBand/associatedMusicalArtist"]
        genre_fusion = [t for t in group if t.predicate in ("musicFusionGenre", "musicSubgenre", "stylisticOrigin")]
        other = [t for t in group if t.predicate not in ("associatedBand/associatedMusicalArtist",
                                                       "musicFusionGenre", "musicSubgenre", "stylisticOrigin")]

        parts = []

        # ---------- Associated bands ----------
        if assoc_triples:
            assoc_names = [t.object for t in assoc_triples]
            if len(assoc_names) == 1:
                parts.append(f"{subject} is associated with {assoc_names[0]}")
            else:
                parts.append(f"{subject} is associated with " +
                             ", ".join(assoc_names[:-1]) +
                             f", and {assoc_names[-1]}")

        # ---------- Other attributes ----------
        attr_phrases = []
        for t in other:
            # Special handling for genre to embed its stylistic origin if available
            if t.predicate == "genre":
                genre_obj = t.object
                # Look for a stylisticOrigin triple where the subject is this genre
                origin_triples = [ot for ot in subject_groups.get(genre_obj, []) if ot.predicate == "stylisticOrigin"]
                if origin_triples:
                    origin_obj = origin_triples[0].object
                    phrase = f"{subject} is a {genre_obj} musician which originated from {origin_obj}"
                else:
                    phrase = simple_phrases["genre"](t.subject, t.object)
                attr_phrases.append(phrase)
            else:
                phrase_func = simple_phrases.get(t.predicate)
                if phrase_func:
                    attr_phrases.append(phrase_func(t.subject, t.object))
                else:
                    attr_phrases.append(f"{t.subject} {t.predicate} {t.object}")

        if attr_phrases:
            if len(attr_phrases) == 1:
                parts.append(attr_phrases[0])
            else:
                parts.append(", ".join(attr_phrases[:-1]) + f", and {attr_phrases[-1]}")

        # ---------- Genre‑fusion information ----------
        for t in genre_fusion:
            phrase_func = simple_phrases.get(t.predicate)
            if phrase_func:
                parts.append(phrase_func(t.subject, t.object))
            else:
                parts.append(f"{t.subject} {t.predicate} {t.object}")

        if parts:
            sentences.append(" ".join(parts) + ".")

    # Combine all subject sentences into one paragraph
    return " ".join(sentences)

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
